100% Complimentary Workshop

Federal Benefits
Workshop:
Weber State University

Thursday, April 9th • 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM

Interactive workshop covering Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS), Thrift Savings Plan (TSP), Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB), Federal Employees Group Life Insurance (FEGLI) and Social Security for federal employees.

Ann Werts

Workshop Instructor

15+ years helping federal employees understand their benefits with personalized guidance and interactive Q&A sessions.

Less than 25 seats remaining

Reserve Your Seat

100% complimentary workshop — no cost, no obligation, no sales pitch.

Privacy Protected & No-Obligation Educational Event
We will not sell your information or send unsolicited communications.

Curriculum

Federal Benefits Workshop Agenda

CH. 01

Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS)

Deep dive into your three-part retirement system: Basic Benefit Plan, Social Security, and TSP. Learn pension calculations, survivor benefits, and strategic retirement timing. Interactive Q&A: General questions about FERS strategies and timing.

Pension Calculations Survivor Benefits Retirement Timing
CH. 02

Thrift Savings Plan (TSP)

Master your TSP with contribution strategies, fund selection, withdrawal options, and rollover decisions. Learn about matching contributions and tax implications. Interactive Discussion: General TSP strategies and best practices.

Contributions Investment Strategy Withdrawals
CH. 03

Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB)

Navigate health insurance in retirement, Medicare coordination, and enrollment decisions. Understand premium sharing and coverage continuation. Q&A Focus: General health benefits scenarios and Medicare timing considerations.

Health Benefits Medicare Timing Premium Costs
CH. 04

Federal Employees Group Life Insurance (FEGLI)

Analyze your life insurance options: Basic Life, Option A, Option B, and Option C. Compare FEGLI costs with private alternatives and understand post-retirement coverage. General Review: Learn about FEGLI options and considerations.

Life Insurance Coverage Options Cost Analysis
Training Authorization

Request Paid Time Off to Attend

Federal employees may be eligible to attend using an SF-182 Training Authorization Form if approved by their agency.

SF-182 Form

Federal employees may be eligible to attend through their agency's SF-182 training authorization process if approved as job-related. Use of an SF-182 is optional and not required to register or attend. Approval, when applicable, is determined solely by each agency.

"15+ years helping federal employees understand their benefits."

Your Workshop Instructor

Ann Werts

Ann has 15+ years of experience helping federal employees understand their benefits. Her expertise in federal benefits has provided education and guidance to thousands of government employees both while they're working and as they approach retirement.

Published Author

"FedTelligence 2.0"
The ultimate guide to mastering your federal benefits

What You Need to Know

Attendance is free of charge

Frequently Asked Questions

Is this affiliated with OPM?

No, this is an independent educational seminar. We provide non-biased, fiduciary guidance that is not limited by government handbook scripts.

Can I bring my spouse?

Absolutely. In fact, we encourage it. Retirement is a joint legacy decision, especially regarding FEHB and Survivor Benefits.

Will I be sold financial products?

This session is purely educational. No financial products will be offered or sold during the seminar. Our goal is clarity, not solicitation.

Confirm Registration

Federal Benefits Educational Workshop

Important Disclaimer: This educational workshop is not affiliated with or endorsed by the U.S. Government, Social Security Administration, Office of Personnel Management, or any federal agency. This is an independent educational seminar provided by Capital Wealth, a registered investment advisor. All information presented is for educational purposes only and should not be considered official government guidance.

Fiduciary. Always.
